title: Hoarseness red-flag screening

presenting_history.text: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact dysphonia history section supports onset, duration, impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, smoking or professional-voice context, and clinician-performed ENT or neck assessment without diagnosing the cause.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

red_flags.text: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: The exact escalation and laryngoscopy section was reviewed for red-flag context, but no automatic examination, imaging, or referral action was mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: assessment, escalation, and laryngoscopy

investigations.text: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.
investigations.rationale: The exact education and outcomes section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending laryngoscopy, imaging, medicine, voice therapy, or referral.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: education and outcomes

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: The exact NICE head-and-neck section supports clinician concern when persistent unexplained hoarseness or an unexplained neck lump is documented in recommendation-qualified age context; it does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

source_limitations: Age, duration, voice quality, smoking, alcohol, occupation, recent surgery or intubation, dysphagia, weight loss, pain, airway symptoms, neck mass, examination, laryngoscopy, diagnosis, investigation, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
